chapter_title.en: What Defiles Clothing and the Body

text: 153 - وَ سَأَلَ زَيْدٌ اَلشَّحَّامُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: عَنِ اَلثَّوْبِ يَكُونُ فِيهِ اَلْجَنَابَةُ وَ تُصِيبُنِي اَلسَّمَاءُ حَتَّى يَبْتَلَّ عَلَيَّ فَقَالَ «لاَ بَأْسَ بِهِ.

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.153 - Zayd al-Shahham asked Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) about a garment that has janabah (ritual impurity due to seminal discharge) and is then rained on, causing it to become wet while he is wearing it.
Imam (as) said: 'There is no harm in it.'
